title: Ship stacked Zellij layout
description: Add a repo-owned Zellij layout that starts Yazelix Next with a stacked tiled pane policy, and make yzn launch the Yazelix Zellij fork with that packaged layout.
acceptance_criteria:
layout.kdl is a repo-owned KDL file.
yzn launches the Yazelix Zellij fork with --layout pointing at the package-owned layout.kdl.
The layout uses Zellij native stacked pane semantics for tiled panes.
The installed package includes share/yazelix-next/layout.kdl.
nix flake show, Zellij layout/config checks, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
notes: Runtime fix applied after dogfood failure: replaced --layout with --new-session-with-layout so yzn launched from inside an existing Zellij session starts a child session in Mars instead of adding a parent-session tab; changed NewPane "Stacked" to NewPane "stacked" to match this fork's accepted action argument. Verified with nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, live profile refresh, and wrapper/config inspection. Did not launch Zellij again from Codex after user reported parent-session tab side effects; awaiting user dogfood.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms yzn opens well after switching to --new-session-with-layout and NewPane "stacked"; verification and profile refresh passed.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Reopened: Runtime fix: yzn did not open correctly from inside an existing Zellij session because --layout adds a tab to the parent session, and NewPane "Stacked" is rejected by this fork. Switch to --new-session-with-layout and lowercase NewPane "stacked".

title: Fix Ctrl Alt key-hint merging in the Zellij bar
description:
Context from investigation on June 25, 2026:

The reported screenshots were from yazelix-next, not main Yazelix. The bottom Zellij status-bar key hints visually merge with a shared modifier prefix when using simple Ctrl bindings, but with Ctrl Alt bindings the row expands into separate full chords such as `Ctrl Alt g > LOCK`, `Ctrl Alt p > PANE`, `Ctrl Alt t > TAB`, etc. The desired behavior is the compact merged shape, without renaming tabs or encoding tab decorations into tab names.

Relevant user-visible symptom:
- Ctrl-only mode hints render compactly as a shared prefix plus per-action keys.
- Ctrl Alt mode hints render each action as a separate full chord, wasting width and making the bar visually noisy.
- Separate related concern: activity dots can sometimes remain forever until the user focuses the tab, even after the pane that generated the activity has stopped. Track/verify this if the chosen bar implementation touches tab activity indicators.

Sources checked:
- Zellij modifier documentation: https://zellij.dev/documentation/changing-modifiers.html
- Zellij key-tooltips announcement/reference: https://zellij.dev/news/web-client-multiple-pane-actions/#key-tooltips
- zjstatus-hints plugin: https://github.com/b0o/zjstatus-hints

Findings:
- Zellij supports changing primary/secondary modifiers, including multi-modifier chords such as Ctrl Alt, when the terminal can report them. The official docs do not expose a setting for status-bar hint joining/formatting.
- This looks like a rendering issue in the built-in Zellij one-line status-bar hint renderer, not a keybind parsing issue.
- In the Zellij status-bar source, the one-line renderer computes common modifiers across all mode-indicator shortcuts, not only the row/mode currently being rendered. That means Ctrl-only can be extracted as a common prefix, while Ctrl Alt can fail to merge if another mode's exit/toggle key lacks Alt.
- Relevant upstream/fork source paths from the earlier inspection:
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/one_line_ui.rs`
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/first_line.rs`
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/main.rs`
- In `one_line_ui.rs`, the relevant flow was:
  - `render_mode_key_indicators(...)`
  - `common_modifiers_in_all_modes(&default_keys)`
  - `render_common_modifiers(...)`
  - fallback through full per-shortcut rendering when no common modifier survives
- Likely small fix: compute common modifiers only for the currently rendered mode's visible shortcuts, then render one shared prefix such as `Ctrl-Alt +` and stripped per-action keys. Add a regression test where Normal-mode indicators all use Ctrl Alt, while a non-current mode has only Ctrl or a different exit shortcut; the rendered Normal row should still use the shared Ctrl Alt prefix.

Alternative path:
- `zjstatus-hints` can feed Zellij keybinding hints into `zjstatus` through a named pipe and may avoid the built-in status-bar renderer entirely.
- That route is larger: it adds plugin/pipe/vendoring/permission/update-path complexity and may have limited formatting configurability. It is worth considering only if yazelix-next chooses a zjstatus-owned bar surface rather than a small Zellij fork fix.

Decision pressure:
- Do not solve this by renaming tabs.
- Prefer a clean ownership choice: either fix the Zellij fork's status-bar renderer, or intentionally replace the built-in bar with a zjstatus/zjstatus-hints style surface.
- For the smallest yazelix-next slice, first evaluate a focused Zellij fork patch plus a regression test before rebuilding the bar from first principles.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ctrl Alt default hints in yazelix-next render with a shared modifier prefix instead of repeating full chords per action.
- The fix does not require changing tab names.
- If the implementation affects tab activity markers, verify that stale activity dots clear without requiring focus on the tab.
- The chosen ownership boundary is recorded: Zellij fork renderer fix, zjstatus-hints integration, or first-party replacement.
notes: Runtime correction on June 25, 2026: the source fix at yazelix-zellij 5769da50 was not enough because Zellij embeds the status-bar plugin WASM from zellij-utils/assets/plugins/status-bar.wasm. Regenerated and pushed the embedded asset in yazelix-zellij 10c79f7e, then updated yazelix-next flake.lock to 10c79f7e and rebuilt .#yzn from the normal locked flake.
close_reason: Completed: fixed in yazelix-zellij commit 5769da50 by deriving one-line mode-hint common modifiers from the currently visible row instead of all modes. Added status-bar regression coverage for Ctrl-Alt hints with another mode using Ctrl. Pushed the fork, updated yazelix-next flake.lock to 5769da50, verified cargo test -p status-bar, nix build .#yzn with override, normal nix build .#yzn, contracts check, flake show --all-systems, and refreshed installed yzn.

title: Support grouped modifier clusters in the Zellij bar
description: The one-line Zellij status bar currently supports at most one shared modifier prefix for the visible mode-hint row. If high-frequency/default Zellij keys such as Ctrl p, Ctrl t, and Ctrl q stay on plain Ctrl while the rest use Ctrl Alt, the renderer should show readable modifier clusters instead of degrading into one noisy mixed row. Keep the key-remap decision separate unless the selected slice explicitly pairs this with the Ctrl p/t/q remap.
design: Code context from yazelix-zellij/default-plugins/status-bar/src/one_line_ui.rs: render_mode_key_indicators currently has exactly two paths: one shared modifier prefix for the whole visible mode-hint row, or no shared prefix. common_modifiers_for_shortcuts now derives that one prefix from the current row for yazelix-next-1mb, which fixes all-Ctrl-Alt rows but cannot cleanly display mixed Ctrl and Ctrl-Alt rows. add_shortcut_with_inline_key and add_shortcut_with_key_only already render stripped keys after a shared prefix, so grouped rendering can reuse them. Better target shape: group rendered hints by modifier set, with a stable group order of Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, then Alt. Within each group, preserve the relative action order from the source row. For a Ctrl p/t/q keymap, the mode row should render as a Ctrl group for PANE/TAB/QUIT and a Ctrl Alt group for LOCK/RESIZE/SEARCH/SESSION. Alt is reserved for yzn-level hints such as Alt m and Alt Shift h if those are brought into the same surface.
acceptance_criteria: Mixed modifier rows render readable modifier groups: Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, and Alt where present; within each group, action order is stable; regression coverage includes Ctrl p/t/q plus Ctrl Alt for the remaining normal-mode hints; existing all-Ctrl-Alt single-prefix behavior from yazelix-next-1mb still passes; no tab-name hacks or zjstatus replacement.
notes: Recommendation: worth doing if we choose Ctrl p/t/q. The status bar should group by modifier set rather than preserve the original interleaved row literally. That keeps the visual model simple: Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, Alt.
close_reason: Completed: grouped modifier clusters and compact secondary hints shipped through child commit addea0e9 and parent commit f7a43ab. Manual dogfooding confirms the status bar is good.

title: Enable Yazi zoxide history updates
description: Shift+Z in Yazi uses Yazi's built-in zoxide preset. Enable its update_db setup so directories visited inside the packaged Yazi sidebar also populate zoxide history.
close_reason: Fixed by enabling Yazi's built-in zoxide preset with update_db in the packaged init.lua. Verified flake show, yzn build, temp profile install, and refreshed installed runtime.

title: Adopt standalone Yazelix bar foundation
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next should eventually consume `yazelix-zellij-bar` because the bar is core to the Yazelix experience, but the first slice should be the standalone generic bar foundation rather than the full main-Yazelix status system. This keeps the Next architecture small while giving future widgets a stable visual and packaging base.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:3`: Yazelix Next is currently a small Nix/Lix flake with one user-facing command, `yzn`.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:23`: `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, and `layout.swap.kdl` currently own Zellij behavior and layout.
- `layout.kdl:2`: the current top bar is native `plugin location="tab-bar"`.
- `layout.kdl:6`: the current bottom bar is native `plugin location="status-bar"` and should keep owning key hints for this slice.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:5`: `yazelix_zellij_bar` is the standalone package for the Yazelix-branded Zellij status-bar preset.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:30`: the standalone bar has no central config framework; KDL is the public surface.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:45`: the generic default must not require `yzx`, `yzx_control`, pane orchestrator, Yazelix caches, Nushell, tokenusage, or full Yazelix.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/status_bar_ownership.md:5`: full Yazelix status-bar ownership is split across zjstatus, the child bar package, runtime adapters, and the pane orchestrator, which is too much scope for the first Next slice.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add `github:luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-bar` as an external flake input.
- Consume the child package as a packaged artifact, not copied Rust/KDL source.
- Replace the native top `tab-bar` pane in `layout.kdl` templates with the child standalone/generic `zjstatus` plugin block.
- Keep the native bottom `status-bar` for Zellij key hints.
- Add a focused contract check that the packaged layout uses the child bar plugin and still contains the native bottom status bar.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard for the new runtime behavior.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after the flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not adopt `render-yazelix-runtime` yet.
- Do not add workspace, activity, sidebar/editor fact, provider usage, CPU, RAM, version, or custom command widgets in this slice.
- Do not add ratconfig, a bar settings file, a bar CLI surface, Home Manager options, or generated user config.
- Do not copy `yazelix-zellij-bar` Rust or preset files into this repo unless package consumption is impossible.
- Do not replace the bottom native Zellij `status-bar` in this slice.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add the `yazelix-zellij-bar` flake input and make it follow this repo's `nixpkgs` when practical.
2. Reference `yazelixZellijBar.packages.${system}.yazelix_zellij_bar` from `flake.nix`.
3. Materialize a layout-owned top bar plugin block from the child package output, preserving this repo's `@yazi@` substitutions and existing swap layout behavior.
4. Keep the bottom `plugin location="status-bar"` unchanged so the compact modifier-hint work remains visible.
5. Extend the existing layout/config contract check or add a small Rust check only if needed; prefer validating observable packaged layout properties over brittle string trivia.
6. Dogfood manually only after the build checks pass, because visual correctness is the real final signal for this slice.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the bar/layout contract check.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the layout with the standalone Yazelix bar at the top and native status bar at the bottom.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime after the package changes.
- Manual `yzn` dogfooding should show the Yazelix-branded top bar while preserving bottom key hints and the existing Yazi/sidebar layout behavior.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if `yazelix-zellij-bar` cannot be consumed as a package artifact without copying child source into this repo.
- Stop if the implementation requires adopting workspace/activity/provider widgets to get a usable top bar.
- Stop if replacing the top bar breaks sidebar swap layout behavior or bottom key-hint visibility.
- Stop if the child bar package contract has drifted materially from the cited standalone/generic boundary.

## Maintenance Notes

- This is a foundation bead. Add widgets later only when each widget has a clear owner, contract, and value.
- The preferred order after this slice is cheap standalone widgets first, provider widgets later, and Yazelix-specific workspace/activity facts last.
- The rejected direction is importing the full main-Yazelix integrated status system into Next before Next has a real status/event owner.

title: Load selected Yazi sidebar plugins
description: Load the selected Yazi plugins for the packaged sidebar: auto-layout, git, and starship. Exclude sidebar-state, sidebar-status, lazygit, and zoxide-editor because they depend on wider Yazelix orchestration or extra tool behavior.
close_reason: Implemented selected Yazi sidebar plugins using imported sources, not vendored plugin Lua: auto-layout.yazi from luccahuguet/auto-layout.yazi, starship.yazi from upstream, and Yazelix-patched git.yazi plus yazelix_starship.toml from a sparse fetch of yazelix-yazi-assets. Excluded sidebar-state, sidebar-status, lazygit, and zoxide-editor. Verified flake show, build, built config inspection, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Restore Zellij bars in stacked layout
description: The custom stacked layout dropped Zellij's default tab-bar and status-bar plugin panes. Restore them while keeping the stacked main pane.
acceptance_criteria:
layout.kdl includes a top tab-bar plugin pane.
layout.kdl includes a bottom status-bar plugin pane.
The main pane remains stacked.
The installed package includes the restored layout.
nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms the restored Zellij bars look good; layout restores tab-bar and status-bar plugin panes while keeping the stacked main pane; package build, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Add a runtime contract matrix
description:
## Why

The architecture doc names owners and tradeoffs, but it does not yet give future agents a compact matrix of runtime contracts and their checks. A matrix would make it obvious which surfaces are protected, manually dogfooded, or still unchecked.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:14-43`: names owners for flake, Mars, Zellij, Nushell, Yazi, yzn-open, and layout checks.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:80-85`: calls out hard dependencies and integration-heavy Yazi behavior as current risks.
- `AGENTS.md:15-20`: asks agents to state behavior, choose one owner, and add focused validation.

## Scope

In scope:
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`
- Possibly `README.md` if a short pointer is useful.

Out of scope:
- Implementing missing checks.
- Changing runtime behavior.
- Adding broad compatibility policies.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a table with columns like Contract, Owner, Check, Manual dogfood, Missing coverage.
2. Cover at least: `yzn` launch, Mars config, Zellij layout/swap, Nushell layering, Yazi opener, Helix bridge, desktop entry, session/window isolation.
3. Mark unchecked contracts plainly rather than pretending they are covered.
4. Link unchecked items to existing Beads where one exists, such as session/window isolation or Yazi config management.
5. Keep the table compact; it should help agents choose validation, not become a design document dump.

## Verification

- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `wc -l .gitignore AGENTS.md README.md ARCHITECTURE.md flake.nix mars.toml config.kdl layout.kdl layout.swap.kdl nu/config.nu nu/env.nu helix/config.toml yazi/init.lua yazi/plugins/sidebar-status.yazi/main.lua yazi/yazi.toml crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs checks/zellij-layout.rs runtime/yzn-nu.rs` should match the README scorecard if docs change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the matrix starts prescribing new features instead of documenting current contracts.
- Stop if the table cannot be verified from source and existing Beads.

## Maintenance Notes

The matrix should make the agent path obvious: contract first, existing owner second, cheapest check third.
close_reason: Completed: ARCHITECTURE.md includes a compact runtime contract matrix covering yzn launch, Mars config, Zellij layout/swap, keybindings, Nushell layering, Yazi opener, Helix bridge, desktop entry, checks, and missing coverage. LOC scorecard updated.

title: Add lightweight CHANGELOG.md
description:
Add a small CHANGELOG.md so user-visible runtime changes are recorded outside chat history.

Acceptance criteria:
- Create a lightweight CHANGELOG.md format that fits the repo's minimal architecture, likely an Unreleased section plus dated release sections only when needed.
- Capture current major user-visible slices at a high level without turning it into release bureaucracy.
- Add a short maintainer rule only if needed: update the changelog when behavior, commands, keymaps, packaged tools, or runtime contracts change.
- Update the README LOC scorecard if project files change.

Rejected direction: do not add release automation, generated changelogs, or versioning machinery until the user chooses that scope.
close_reason: Completed: added lightweight CHANGELOG.md, documented the maintainer update rule, refreshed the README LOC scorecard, and verified the docs diff and LOC totals.

title: Adopt contract-driven check-backed development
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next is fully agent-driven, so project intent must live in durable repo state rather than chat history. The current repo already points in this direction, but the method is not yet explicit enough for future agents.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:13-20`: requires contract distillation, one repo owner, focused validation, and recording rejected alternatives in Beads.
- `AGENTS.md:48-49`: says important decisions should be kept in Beads instead of chat history.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:89-94`: says the main simplicity win is one owner per contract and no duplicated compatibility surface.
- `README.md:59-73`: the repo tracks owned LOC, so process changes should stay visible and justified.

## Scope

In scope:
- Track the small implementation program for adopting contract-driven, check-backed development.
- Keep the method compatible with AI-agent-first development.

Out of scope:
- Implement feature work.
- Add broad process machinery or templates that create more overhead than clarity.

## Implementation Notes

1. Complete the child beads that document the method, expose reusable checks, and map current contracts to checks.
2. Close this epic when those child beads are done or explicitly rejected.

## Verification

- `br dep tree $EPIC` should show the child work.
- `br ready` should keep implementation beads independently actionable.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the user chooses a different development method.
- Stop if a child bead would require implementing unrelated runtime features.

## Maintenance Notes

The intended method is contract-driven and check-backed. Use TDD only where it is the cheapest way to prove a small deterministic helper or regression.
close_reason: Completed: child beads documented the workflow, added flake contract checks, and added the architecture contract matrix.

title: Decide yzn key remaps
description:
Decide the small first-class key remap set for yzn before adding more bindings. Current owned Zellij config remaps Alt n to Alt m for new stacked panes and uses Alt Shift h to show/hide the sidebar.

Acceptance criteria:
- List the remaps yzn should own, including current Alt m and Alt Shift h behavior.
- Compare against main Yazelix muscle memory, Zellij defaults, Mars/window-manager conflicts, and Yazi/Helix/Nushell keys.
- Mark each candidate as keep, add, change, or reject, with a short reason.
- Choose one owner for each accepted remap, normally config.kdl unless another layer is clearly better.
- Do not implement remaps in this bead unless the user explicitly chooses the final set.
close_reason: Decided and implemented the first yzn key policy: Ctrl Alt owns native Zellij mode control for lock/pane/tab/resize/search/session/quit, Ctrl Alt h/j/k/l owns direct tab/pane movement, Alt m remains new stacked pane, Alt Shift h remains sidebar toggle, and Move mode is intentionally unbound. Packaged config is now covered by yzn-contracts.

title: Document the contract-driven agent workflow
description:
## Why

The repo has the seeds of the workflow, but future agents need the final method written in current-state project instructions: contract first, one owner, smallest implementation, focused check, rejected alternatives in Beads. This prevents generic TDD, broad compatibility surfaces, and mechanical ports from main Yazelix.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:13-20`: contract distillation exists but does not name the final method or explain when TDD is appropriate.
- `AGENTS.md:61-68`: verification guidance exists but is command-oriented, not tied to feature contracts.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:89-94`: architecture doc already frames one owner per contract as the real simplicity win.

## Scope

In scope:
- `AGENTS.md`
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`
- A short README pointer only if it improves discoverability.

Out of scope:
- Source code changes.
- New feature work.
- A heavyweight process template file.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a concise section naming the method: contract-driven, check-backed development.
2. Define the loop: contract, owner, check, implementation, verification, rejected alternatives.
3. State when TDD is useful: Rust helpers, parsers, regressions, deterministic CLI behavior.
4. State when TDD is not the default: layout design, runtime integration, fork decisions, dogfooding surfaces.
5. Keep wording terse enough for agents to follow quickly.

## Verification

- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `wc -l .gitignore AGENTS.md README.md ARCHITECTURE.md flake.nix mars.toml config.kdl layout.kdl layout.swap.kdl nu/config.nu nu/env.nu helix/config.toml yazi/init.lua yazi/plugins/sidebar-status.yazi/main.lua yazi/yazi.toml crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs checks/zellij-layout.rs runtime/yzn-nu.rs` should match the README scorecard if docs change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if documenting the method would require choosing new product scope.
- Stop if the docs become a generic process essay instead of actionable agent guidance.

## Maintenance Notes

Do not call the default process TDD. The stronger name is contract-driven, check-backed development.
close_reason: Completed: AGENTS.md names contract-driven, check-backed development; states when TDD fits and when it is not the default; verification guidance includes nix flake check. Verified git diff --check, LOC scorecard, nix flake show, nix flake check, nix build, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Use Nushell as default pane shell
description: Make the packaged Yazelix Next Zellij config start panes with Nushell as the default shell, while keeping the change small and reversible.
acceptance_criteria:
config.kdl declares default_shell using a Nix-substituted Nushell path.
yzn runtime includes Nushell on PATH.
The packaged share/yazelix-next/config.kdl contains an absolute /nix/store nu path.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms Nushell default shell is acceptable; flake show, build, temp profile install, live profile refresh, and config/wrapper inspection passed.

title: Expose flake checks for runtime contracts
description:
## Why

Agent-driven development needs one deterministic command that proves the core runtime contracts without launching a GUI. The repo currently has packages/apps and build-time guards, but no `checks` output that an agent can run as the default contract suite.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:61-68`: runtime changes normally require `nix flake show --all-systems`, `nix build .#yzn`, and a profile install check.
- `flake.nix:39-44`: local Rust helpers are compiled inside Nix.
- `flake.nix:145-149`: `yzn-layout-check` validates the Zellij layout during the package build.
- `runtime/yzn-nu.rs:24-49`: `yzn-nu` owns generated Nushell config layering and exec behavior, but its smoke test is manual.

## Scope

In scope:
- `flake.nix`
- `checks/` or small Nix check derivations
- Existing Rust helpers if exposing checks requires tiny adjustments.

Out of scope:
- GUI launch tests.
- Home Manager or desktop environment test infrastructure.
- Broad CI setup unless the user separately chooses it.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add `checks.${system}` outputs for cheap runtime contracts.
2. Include at least: package build, Zellij layout template/swap validation, and `yzn-nu` config layering with a temporary config root.
3. Keep checks non-interactive and independent from the user's real home/config.
4. Prefer Nix derivations that execute the exact built artifacts over separate mock implementations.
5. Document the chosen command in `AGENTS.md` or README if it becomes the recommended default.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 on the current system.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should still exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- README LOC scorecard should be updated if owned files change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if a check requires opening Mars or a graphical Zellij session.
- Stop if the check duplicates large runtime behavior instead of executing the built helper.
- Stop if `nix flake check` becomes so heavy that agents avoid running it.

## Maintenance Notes

This bead is about agent affordance: one obvious command should prove the contracts that currently require remembering ad hoc commands from chat.
close_reason: Completed: flake.nix exposes checks for yzn and contracts. nix flake check builds yzn and runs a non-GUI yzn-nu temporary-config smoke test that proves packaged config, user layering, carapace, and zoxide. Verified nix flake check and nix build .#yzn.

title: Design minimal Yazi config management
description:
## Context

Yazelix Next now uses Yazi as the left file-manager sidebar. We want users to be happy with real file-manager behavior: copy/cut/paste files and folders, previews, zoxide/fzf, search, open-with behavior, visual selection, bulk operations, themes/icons, and normal Yazi ergonomics. That makes Yazi the pragmatic file-manager choice for now.

The risk is config management. Main Yazelix handles Yazi config through a full managed materialization system: Rust adapter/writer, generated runtime config, TOML/keymap merging, user init appending, managed plugin/flavor import, legacy path rejection, and a separate yazi-assets renderer. Current measured related surfaces were roughly 2.5k LOC in main Yazelix Yazi materialization/docs/plugins, 1.1k LOC in shared import/path/stub support, and 8k LOC in yazelix-yazi-assets. Do not recreate that shape in Yazelix Next without a very strong reason.

## Direction

Keep the boundary simple:

- Yazi owns file management.
- Yazelix Next owns workspace integration.
- Packaged Yazi config owns only the baseline behavior needed for the sidebar to feel good out of the box.
- User customization should be added only when there is a concrete workflow need, not as a generic config-management project.

Preferred future shape, if/when needed:

1. Keep repo-owned packaged Yazi config tiny and explicit.
2. Avoid TOML deep-merge and generated config directories unless forced by a real use case.
3. Prefer narrow extension points over a broad managed Yazi home.
4. If layering is needed, start with the smallest safe surface, probably Lua `init.lua` after packaged setup, then reassess.
5. Record any irreversible ownership decisions before implementation.

## Rejected Or Deferred Alternatives

- Fork Yazi to remove glue: rejected as a default. It inherits upstream churn and still needs Yazelix integration policy.
- Fork Elio now: deferred. Elio is worth evaluating, but forking before proving it is simpler than a tiny bridge is premature.
- Roll our own terminal file manager: rejected for the user-happiness target because expected file-manager features are broad.
- Zellij-plugin file manager: deferred as a long-term idea; if pursued, keep the core independent from Zellij with a Zellij adapter, but do not replace Yazi now.
- Bite the bullet and port main Yazelix materialization: rejected for Yazelix Next simplicity unless future requirements clearly justify it.

## Acceptance Criteria For A Future Implementation

- State the exact user-visible workflow that requires config management.
- Prove why packaged defaults are not enough.
- Add the smallest config surface that solves that workflow.
- Keep the README LOC scorecard honest.
- Do not introduce generated config directories, TOML merging, plugin import, migration logic, or compatibility shims unless each has a concrete acceptance criterion.
- Keep runtime validation focused on observable Yazi behavior, not generic config plumbing.

title: Add guarded Zellij native config sidecar
description:
## Why

Zellij preferences should be user-editable, but a full native Zellij config replacement would let user config break the Yazelix Next runtime spine. The current packaged config owns the managed shell, key remaps, pane creation key, and sidebar toggle, so the first user config surface must be a guarded native sidecar for safe preferences only.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:83`: the packaged Zellij config is generated from repo-owned `config.kdl` with the managed Nushell path substituted in.
- `flake.nix:187`: `yzn` launches Zellij with `--config ${yznConfigKdl}` and `--new-session-with-layout ${yznZellijLayout}/layout.kdl`.
- `config.kdl:1`: `default_shell` is managed and points at the packaged `yzn-nu` shell.
- `config.kdl:3`: `keybinds` is managed by Yazelix Next.
- `config.kdl:31`: `Alt m` is the managed new-pane binding used with the layout behavior.
- `config.kdl:32`: `Alt Shift h` is the managed sidebar swap binding.
- `layout.kdl:1`: layout ownership is separate from config and currently defines the managed tab/sidebar/status-bar structure.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:23`: `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, and `layout.swap.kdl` are documented Zellij behavior owners.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add an optional guarded sidecar at `~/.config/yazelix-next/zellij/config.kdl`.
- Build the runtime Zellij config from the packaged `config.kdl` plus the user sidecar only when the sidecar passes validation.
- Treat the sidecar as native Zellij preferences, not a full Zellij config replacement.
- Fail fast with a clear message when the sidecar contains dangerous top-level nodes or settings that would replace Yazelix-owned behavior.
- Add a focused contract check for accepted safe sidecar content and rejected dangerous content.
- Document the sidecar path, allowed intent, and forbidden ownership boundaries in `README.md` and `ARCHITECTURE.md`.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` and the README LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after changing the flake runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not allow user replacement of `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, or `layout.swap.kdl`.
- Do not support arbitrary native Zellij keymaps in this sidecar.
- Do not support layout ownership, plugin alias ownership, default shell ownership, default layout ownership, session startup ownership, or pane policy ownership through this sidecar.
- Do not add semantic key remaps, profiles, ratconfig, Home Manager, or a CLI config editor in this slice.
- Do not silently ignore dangerous config. Reject it so the user knows why the sidecar is not being used.

## Implementation Notes

1. Define the allowed/forbidden sidecar contract before coding. Start strict and widen later only with concrete user value.
2. Prefer a small Rust validator if text checks would be brittle. The validator does not need a complete KDL semantic model; it only needs to reject forbidden top-level ownership nodes/settings reliably enough for the current contract.
3. Initial forbidden top-level items should include at least `keybinds`, `default_shell`, `default_layout`, `layout`, `plugins`, `load_plugins`, and any other node that clearly takes ownership of startup, layout, shell, plugin loading, or key policy.
4. Generate a runtime config under `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR` or another launch-local runtime directory by concatenating packaged config and validated sidecar content.
5. Launch Zellij with the generated runtime config path when a sidecar exists; otherwise keep using the packaged config path.
6. Keep layout and swap layout packaging unchanged.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include sidecar validation contract coverage.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the sidecar-aware launcher/config helper.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime.
- Manual dogfooding, when requested, should show safe sidecar preferences applying without losing the managed shell, `Alt m`, `Alt Shift h`, sidebar layout, or bottom status-bar key hints.
- A sidecar containing `keybinds` or `default_shell` should fail before launching Zellij and print a clear diagnostic naming the forbidden node.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij sidecar support requires a full native Zellij config merge framework.
- Stop if the implementation cannot fail clearly on dangerous ownership nodes.
- Stop if supporting a requested preference requires touching managed layout, managed keymaps, or plugin orchestration; create a separate bead instead.
- Stop if the validator grows into a broad Zellij schema or semantic keybinding system.

## Maintenance Notes

- This sidecar is intentionally stricter than Mars config override because Zellij config owns the Yazelix Next runtime spine.
- Future semantic key remaps should be a separate owner-scoped config surface, not arbitrary native `keybinds` in this file.
- Rejected direction: letting `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` or a full user-owned Zellij config become the default for managed `yzn` sessions.

title: Adopt Mars Yazelix config
description: Wire yzn to a package-owned Mars config root built from mars.toml, with Yazelix font settings, hidden window decorations, and reef split cursor colors.
acceptance_criteria:
yzn launches Mars with MARS_CONFIG_HOME pointing at a package-owned config root.
mars.toml owns the Mars settings, uses the Yazelix Mars font stack, disables window decorations, and hardcodes reef cursor colors using Mars-supported split cursor fields.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, and a temp profile install pass.
Installed yzn profile is refreshed.
notes: Implemented mars.toml as the yzn-owned Mars config. Verification: `nix flake show --all-systems` passed; `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` passed; TOML parse check confirmed confirm=true, bell audio=true, bell visual=true, font=JetBrains Mono, decorations=Disabled; built wrapper exports MARS_CONFIG_HOME=/nix/store/dqa1ldd7lnnwbdphf9ymhv3sa6nnb63z-yzn-mars-config; temp profile install exposed bin/yzn; `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` refreshed installed yzn to /nix/store/gp9l80zvz3skqzhn75wsylzs8x3r38b0-yzn. Leave open until human dogfooding confirms the Mars window looks right.
close_reason: Completed: yzn uses the yzn-owned Mars config with JetBrains Mono, hidden window decorations, reef cursor colors, confirm-before-quit, both bells enabled, verified by Nix checks and user dogfooding.

title: Make yzn-open logging bounded or disableable
description:
yzn-open currently appends diagnostic events to `${YAZELIX_STATE_DIR}/logs/yzn-open.log` with no rotation or disable switch. This was useful for debugging Yazi-to-Helix opens, but it should not become an unbounded always-on log surface.

Acceptance criteria:
- Keep default diagnostics small and bounded, either with simple rotation/truncation or a clear opt-in debug mode.
- Provide a way to disable or minimize yzn-open logging without changing source code.
- Preserve enough failure evidence for Yazi open debugging when explicitly enabled.
- Keep the implementation simple and avoid introducing a logging framework unless the need becomes real.
close_reason: Implemented bounded yzn-open diagnostics: added YZN_OPEN_LOG levels off/error/info/debug, default info routing logs, debug-only payload/registry details, error-only failure logs, 64 KiB rotation with one yzn-open.log.1 backup, README/architecture docs, and tests for parsing, disable/error filtering, and rotation. Verified with cargo test, nix flake check, nix flake show, nix build, temp profile install, and nix profile upgrade.

title: Layer yzn Nushell user config
description: Allow yzn-scoped Nushell config layering without touching normal Nushell config. Packaged env/config should load first, then optional files under ~/.config/yazelix-next/nu via wrapper-generated runtime Nu source files.
close_reason: Implemented yzn-scoped Nushell config layering with packaged files first and optional user env/config files loaded from ~/.config/yazelix-next/nu via scripts/yzn-nu.sh.

title: Launch the Yazelix Zellij fork under Mars
description:
## Context
Extend the tiny Yazelix Next launcher so `yzn` opens Mars running the Yazelix Zellij fork instead of a plain shell. The fork is the temporary Yazelix KGP/APC passthrough Zellij source consumed by main Yazelix, not upstream nixpkgs Zellij.

## Decisions
- Keep this in the flake/package surface only.
- Consume the Yazelix Zellij fork as a non-flake source input.
- Do not add Home Manager, layouts, config generation, plugins, or pane policy in this slice.
- Preserve the per-language LOC scorecard.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `flake.nix` has a `yazelixZellij` non-flake input pointing at the Yazelix fork branch.
- `packages.${system}.yazelix_zellij` packages that fork from source using nixpkgs' Zellij packaging as the base where possible.
- `yzn` launches Mars with the packaged Yazelix Zellij fork as its command.
- `nix run` and `nix run .#yzn` still use the same launcher app surface.
- README says `yzn` opens Mars running the Yazelix Zellij fork.
- README documents `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` as the installed-runtime update command.
- The per-language LOC scorecard is updated after the Nix and README changes.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` succeeds.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` succeeds or any build blocker is recorded with the exact failing package/dependency.
- A temp profile install using `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` exposes `<tmp>/bin/yzn`.
- The user's installed `yazelix-next` profile entry is refreshed after runtime changes.
- Human dogfooding confirms `yzn` opens a Mars window running the Yazelix Zellij fork before the bead is closed.
notes: Profile refresh evidence: ran `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next`; `nix profile list` now shows `yazelix-next` at `/nix/store/7imysps2q66y7aimv3xv6963hi6g3xhm-yzn`, and `/home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/yzn` execs Mars with `/nix/store/3frryp6kl6sk7pqwcc0knksyf65411sa-zellij-0.44.3/bin/zellij`.
close_reason: Completed: yzn launches Mars with the packaged Yazelix Zellij fork; nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, zellij --version, profile refresh, and user dogfooding all passed.

title: Add Yazi sidebar pane
description: Add a minimal left Yazi pane to the packaged Zellij layout using packaged yazi, while keeping Yazi config layering out of scope unless needed.
close_reason: Implemented a minimal packaged Yazi sidebar: layout.kdl has a 20% left command pane using a Nix-substituted yazi path, main pane remains stacked, no Yazi config/user layering added. Verified flake show, build, built layout inspection, Yazi store reference, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Add minimal Starship config surface
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next currently hardwires Starship into packaged Nushell config, while user prompt customization requires overriding Nushell prompt variables after packaged setup. That works but can create stacked prompt markers and does not give users a clean Starship-native way to tune the prompt. A minimal Starship config surface would let users visually customize the prompt while keeping Nushell layering and shell startup simple.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:68`: packaged `nu/config.nu` is rendered with the packaged `starship` binary path.
- `flake.nix:80`: `yzn-nu` puts `starship` on `PATH` with the other shell tools.
- `nu/config.nu:4`: `PROMPT_COMMAND` always calls the packaged `starship prompt` command.
- `nu/config.nu:5`: packaged config clears `PROMPT_COMMAND_RIGHT` before user config is sourced.
- `runtime/yzn-nu.rs:27`: user Nushell config is read from `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME/nu`.
- `runtime/yzn-nu.rs:77`: packaged Nushell config is sourced first, then optional user `config.nu` is sourced after it.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a minimal Starship config path for managed `yzn` shells, likely `~/.config/yazelix-next/starship.toml` or `~/.config/yazelix-next/starship/config.toml`.
- Make packaged Nushell/Starship prompt use that Starship config when it exists.
- Keep packaged Starship defaults when the user config file does not exist.
- Document the Starship config path and its relationship to `nu/config.nu`.
- Add a focused contract check that proves the Starship config path is wired without requiring interactive shell rendering.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` and the README LOC scorecard if implementation touches tracked files.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not add ratconfig, Home Manager, a prompt UI, or a broad settings file.
- Do not make `yzn` read the user's normal `~/.config/starship.toml` by default.
- Do not replace Nushell config layering or remove the existing `~/.config/yazelix-next/nu/config.nu` escape hatch.
- Do not design a theme/profile system yet.
- Do not hardcode the current turbofish smoke marker as a product default.

## Implementation Notes

1. Pick one path and document it before coding. Prefer the shortest path that matches existing config-root behavior.
2. Consider setting `STARSHIP_CONFIG` in the runtime Nushell environment when the user Starship config exists; otherwise leave Starship on packaged/default behavior.
3. Keep user `nu/config.nu` able to override prompt variables after packaged setup for advanced cases.
4. If a packaged default Starship config is added, keep it tiny and explicit.
5. Avoid TOML merging. Starship already owns its native config file format.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include any contract check changes.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime.
- A non-interactive `yzn-nu --commands` smoke should prove that a temporary `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME` with the Starship config file causes the runtime to point Starship at that file.
- Manual dogfooding, when requested, should show a visible prompt change in a fresh `yzn` window without stacked prompt indicators.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Starship config support requires prompt rendering logic owned by Yazelix Next.
- Stop if the implementation needs to merge Starship TOML or infer Starship schema details.
- Stop if the cleanest solution conflicts with the existing user Nushell config layering contract.

## Maintenance Notes

- This is a native-file config surface like Mars, but narrower: Starship owns prompt formatting, Nushell owns shell startup, and `yzn` only chooses the config path.
- Rejected direction: using Nushell `PROMPT_INDICATOR` as the main prompt customization surface. It can stack with Starship and is better kept as an advanced user escape hatch.
notes: Implementation uses YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME/starship.toml when present and a readable empty fallback config otherwise, currently /dev/null, so normal ~/.config/starship.toml stays outside the managed runtime without adding a Nix fallback derivation.
close_reason: Implemented minimal Starship config surface: yzn-nu sets STARSHIP_CONFIG to YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME/starship.toml when present and to an empty packaged config otherwise; contracts cover user override and non-inherited fallback; README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard updated; flake check/build/profile install/install smoke passed; installed yzn refreshed.

title: Ship owned Zellij config remap
description: Add a package-owned Zellij config and make yzn launch the Yazelix Zellij fork with it, starting by moving Zellij's native new-pane binding from Alt+n to Alt+m. This deliberately does not pull in the pane-orchestrator plugin yet; Yazelix-style workspace-terminal behavior remains a later slice.
acceptance_criteria:
config.kdl is a repo-owned KDL file.
yzn launches the Yazelix Zellij fork with --config pointing at the package-owned config.kdl.
Alt+n is unbound from the native new-pane default.
Alt+m runs the native Zellij NewPane action for now.
The installed package includes share/yazelix-next/config.kdl.
nix flake show, zellij setup --check with the config, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: yzn packages and uses config.kdl; config unbinds Alt+n and binds Alt+m to the native Zellij NewPane action; zellij setup --check, nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Allow full native Mars config override
description:
## Why

Mars terminal preferences should be user-editable without adding a Yazelix Next settings schema or ratconfig surface. The current runtime always launches Mars with the packaged generated config, which keeps the slice reproducible but makes simple native Mars preferences like opacity, font size, window size, bells, cursor, colors, scrollback, and decorations require repository edits.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:53`: the packaged Mars config is produced only from repo-owned `mars.toml` through `replaceVars`.
- `flake.nix:59`: `yzn-mars-config` installs that packaged config as `config.toml`.
- `flake.nix:186`: `yzn` always exports `MARS_CONFIG_HOME=${yznMarsConfig}` before launching Mars.
- `mars.toml:1`: the repo-owned file already contains native Mars top-level preferences.
- `mars.toml:21`: native Mars window preferences such as size, decorations, opacity, and opacity-cells are currently hardcoded in the package.
- `mars.toml:33`: native Mars font preferences are currently hardcoded in the package.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:20`: `mars.toml` is currently documented as the terminal visual config owner.

## Scope

In scope:
- If `~/.config/yazelix-next/mars/config.toml` exists, launch Mars with `MARS_CONFIG_HOME` pointing at `~/.config/yazelix-next/mars`.
- Otherwise keep launching Mars with the packaged config output.
- Keep `yzn` launch command, Zellij command, environment, editor variables, and session state owned by the launcher.
- Document the user-owned Mars config path in `README.md` and the ownership boundary in `ARCHITECTURE.md`.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` and the README LOC scorecard.
- Add or extend a focused contract check that verifies the fallback packaged path and user-config path selection behavior.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after changing the flake runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not merge packaged and user Mars TOML.
- Do not validate or schema-check the user's full Mars TOML beyond relying on Mars itself to parse it.
- Do not introduce ratconfig, Home Manager, generated settings, or a CLI surface for Mars config.
- Do not expose individual Mars options as Nix options or Yazelix settings.
- Do not let Mars config own the `yzn -> Mars -> Zellij` startup command.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a small launcher-level config selection in `yznCommand`: derive `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME` from the existing config-root convention, defaulting to `~/.config/yazelix-next` when unset.
2. If `$YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME/mars/config.toml` exists, export `MARS_CONFIG_HOME=$YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME/mars`; otherwise export `MARS_CONFIG_HOME=${yznMarsConfig}`.
3. Keep the packaged config installed under `share/yazelix-next` if useful for inspection or copying.
4. Prefer shell-level existence checks in the existing `writeShellApplication` unless the launcher logic grows enough to justify a Rust helper.
5. Extend `checks/yzn-contracts.rs` only if it can test the behavior cheaply without launching Mars; otherwise add a small package-output/launcher-text check.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the config-selection contract if implemented there.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the updated launcher.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime.
- Manual dogfooding, when requested, should show that adding `~/.config/yazelix-next/mars/config.toml` changes Mars terminal preferences while `yzn` still starts the managed Zellij runtime.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Mars requires config merging to support this path; whole-file native ownership is the intended contract.
- Stop if a user Mars config can replace the `yzn` startup command or prevent Zellij launch through terminal TOML alone.
- Stop if the implementation needs a broad settings schema, ratconfig, or Home Manager surface.

## Maintenance Notes

- This is intentionally asymmetric with Zellij. Mars config is mostly terminal preference; Zellij config owns parts of the Yazelix runtime spine.
- Rejected direction: hardcoding a small list of Mars options in Yazelix Next. Native Mars TOML is the simpler and more complete user surface.
close_reason: Completed: yzn now uses a user native Mars config at YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME/mars/config.toml when present, falls back to the packaged Mars config, ships a packaged config copy for inspection, updates docs/changelog/LOC, verifies contracts/build/profile install, and refreshes the installed runtime.

title: Launch a minimal host-tool Zellij workspace
close_reason: Obsolete planning: created before the user selected the first scope. The accepted baseline is a flake-installed yzn launcher for Mars with a LOC scorecard, not a Zellij workspace yet.

title: Add slim agent protocol from Yazelix rewrite bead
description:
## Context
Add a slim `AGENTS.md` for Yazelix Next that distills the old Yazelix private-rewrite bead guidance into repo-local rules. The file should guide agents without importing the full main Yazelix policy surface.

## Decisions
- Use `yazelix-9hpmy` as the source planning bead and `yazelix-z9kew` as the merged contract-distillation method.
- Keep the guidance short and specific to this repo.
- Emphasize user-led scope decisions, contract distillation, small vertical slices, LOC discipline, Beads, and exact verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `AGENTS.md` exists and is concise.
- `AGENTS.md` captures contract-distillation rules from old Yazelix beads without copying the full main `AGENTS.md`.
- `AGENTS.md` tells agents to use `br` for Beads and not to invent scope before the user decides.
- `AGENTS.md` documents the current runtime chain and installed `yzn` refresh rule.
- README LOC scorecard includes `AGENTS.md` as Markdown.
- `br lint --json` stays clean.
close_reason: Completed: added slim AGENTS.md distilled from yazelix-9hpmy/yazelix-z9kew guidance and updated README per-language LOC scorecard.

title: Explore Herdr-inspired agent-native runtime model
description:
## Why

Herdr is interesting because it treats the terminal runtime as agent-aware rather than just terminal text. It combines a real multiplexer core, persistent server/client sessions, PTY ownership, workspace/sidebar state, agent detection, socket/API automation, and integrations. Yazelix Next should study that direction before adding more isolated glue, because agent-native behavior could become a core differentiator if we can keep the architecture simple.

## User-Visible Behavior To Distill

A Yazelix Next window should eventually understand which panes are agents, what state they are in, and how that state rolls up into workspace/sidebar/status UI, while keeping real terminal processes and normal user tools intact. Agents should be runtime objects with metadata and automation surfaces, not just text patterns in panes.

## Herdr Ideas To Evaluate

- Persistent server/client model where closing the UI client does not kill pane processes.
- Real PTY ownership for panes, not fake rewritten agent views.
- Serializable workspace/tab/pane/layout/agent metadata separated from runtime objects like PTYs, child handles, readers, writers, and detectors.
- Agent detection through process names and terminal output by default, with socket/API integrations when available.
- State model such as blocked, working, done, idle, and rollups into a sidebar or bar.
- Live server replacement via PTY master file descriptor transfer with SCM_RIGHTS as a future inspiration, not immediate scope.
- libghostty-vt or equivalent terminal-state ownership as a possible long-term primitive.

## Scope

- Compare what Yazelix Next can get by staying on Zellij/Yazi/Helix plus small Rust helpers versus forking deeper versus building or adopting a multiplexer core.
- Identify the smallest agent-native vertical slice that fits the current `yzn -> Mars -> Zellij -> Yazi/Helix` runtime.
- Define which object should own agent identity, state, detection, lifecycle, and automation API in Yazelix Next.
- Decide whether an agent sidebar/bar should consume state from Zellij, from a Yazelix helper daemon, from pane-local detectors, or from future multiplexer ownership.
- Record rejected paths and why.

## Out Of Scope

- Do not build a terminal multiplexer in this bead.
- Do not replace Zellij or Mars as part of this bead.
- Do not add broad socket APIs, daemon state, SCM_RIGHTS handoff, or terminal emulation code without a later implementation bead.
- Do not treat text scraping alone as the final architecture if a cleaner integration surface exists.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Produce a recommendation for the next agent-native slice, ranked by simplicity, user value, and LOC impact.
- Name the surviving Herdr ideas and the ideas deliberately rejected for Yazelix Next.
- Define a minimal state model for agents if one is recommended.
- Identify concrete owners in this repo or external forks for any proposed runtime objects.
- Create follow-up implementation beads only after the user chooses a direction.

## Notes

The strongest architectural lesson is separation of serializable app state from runtime ownership. The immediate Yazelix Next question is not whether Herdr is impressive; it is how much of that agent-native model can be borrowed while preserving the project goal of minimal LOC and simple ownership.
notes:
Investigation 2026-06-25:

Herdr was cloned to /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-related/herdr for local source inspection. Current upstream HEAD is 3c7327c fix: avoid pi release on internal session reloads (#800).

Herdr persistence is primarily server-owned runtime, not config replay. Running herdr starts or attaches to a background server; detaching closes only the client. The server keeps TerminalRuntimeRegistry objects, PTY ownership, readers/writers, detectors, layout state, sockets, and pane processes alive. Session JSON stores serializable shape such as workspaces, tabs, panes, cwd, labels, launch argv, and agent session refs; after a stopped server it restores shells and optional native agent sessions, not arbitrary live process handles.

Live process preservation across server replacement is the expensive part. Herdr live handoff duplicates PTY master fds, sends them to the replacement process with SCM_RIGHTS, imports them as runtime objects, waits for the new server to restore sockets, then old server drains/preserves runtimes and exits. This is a future inspiration only; it does not fit Yazelix Next near-term LOC or complexity goals.

Agent-native state is a clean layered model: TerminalState owns terminal identity, cwd, agent metadata, detected agent, fallback state, hook authority, custom status, and persisted agent session refs. PaneState only attaches view state to a terminal id. Detection is decoupled from the renderer and reads bottom-buffer/screen evidence through manifests. Hook/socket integrations can report stronger semantic state than text scraping. Workspace aggregation prioritizes blocked, unseen done, working, idle, unknown.

Main Yazelix already has a right agent sidebar as a managed terminal role, configurable through workspace.right_sidebar.* and launched by yzx agent. It does not yet have Herdr-style agent-state ownership: identity/state/automation are not durable runtime objects rolled up across sessions.

Yazelix Next currently launches zellij with --new-session-with-layout, so every yzn launch biases toward a fresh generated session. Zellij already has the first-order primitives that Herdr uses at the user level: named sessions, attach --create, list-sessions, kill/delete, detached background creation, on_force_close detach, and session serialization. That means the cheapest detach/attach improvement is to consume Zellij better, not build a server.

Session slice implications for Yazelix Next:

Current yzn identity is top-level-launch-scoped, while detach/attach wants session-scoped identity. Reattaching to an existing Zellij session must not mint a fresh YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID that disagrees with already-running Yazi and Helix panes. Before changing launch behavior, define whether plain yzn means attach/create a stable default Yazelix Next session or always create a new window/session. Layout should be applied when creating or resurrecting a session, not blindly on every attach. The first implementation check should prove command construction and bridge/session identity behavior without launching a GUI. Agent sidebar/state should stay deferred until this attach/detach contract is correct.

Recommendation ranked by simplicity and value:

1. First improve yzn session attach/detach around Zellij. Give yzn an explicit small session surface and stable default session behavior, likely using zellij attach --create plus the packaged layout/config, and add list/stop/delete only if the contract needs them. This directly fixes the user pain without owning PTYs.
2. Then add a tiny agent-state owner only after sessions feel good. The smallest state model should be unknown, idle, working, blocked, done-as-idle-unseen. Owner should probably be a small Yazelix Next helper or future bar input, keyed by Zellij session plus pane id. The sidebar/bar should consume that state; it should not own detection.
3. Defer Herdr-scale pieces: own multiplexer core, PTY ownership, libghostty-vt, socket automation, direct terminal attach, and SCM_RIGHTS live handoff. Revisit only if Zellij cannot support the desired session/mobile/agent workflows.

Rejected for now: replacing Zellij, forking deeper just for persistence, rolling our own terminal file manager or multiplexer, and treating text scraping alone as the final agent-native architecture.

title: Add yzn desktop entry
description: Ship a desktop launcher in the yzn package so profile installs expose Yazelix Next in graphical app launchers without Home Manager.
acceptance_criteria:
packages.${system}.yzn contains bin/yzn and share/applications/yzn.desktop.
The desktop entry launches yzn without a terminal, uses a Yazelix Next name, and installs an icon under the yzn icon name.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, and temp profile install pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
Installed yzn profile is refreshed.
close_reason: Completed: yzn package now ships bin/yzn, share/applications/yzn.desktop, and yzn icons; flake show/build/temp profile install/profile refresh all passed.

title: Simplify yzn runtime plumbing
description: Reduce real LOC and concepts while preserving current yzn behavior: remove fallback/runtime duplication and one-call wrappers where verification proves equivalence.
close_reason: Completed real simplification pass: removed Zellij base compatibility fallback, removed duplicate Nu tool runtime inputs from outer yzn wrapper, and stopped copying generated carapace/zoxide init files into final share output. Rejected prompt inlining because it hurt readability for formatting-style LOC only. Verified flake show, build, direct yzn-nu smoke, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Fix Yazi auto-layout setup
description: The Yazi sidebar loads auto-layout.yazi but does not call setup(), so the layout override is never installed and Yazi still shows multiple columns in the narrow sidebar.
close_reason: Fixed yazi/init.lua to call require("auto-layout"):setup(), matching main Yazelix core-plugin setup behavior so the Tab.layout override is installed. Verified build, built init inspection, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Reuse Helix editor pane from Yazi
description: Make the packaged Yazi opener keep Yazi as the sidebar when creating the first editor pane, then reuse a live Yazelix Helix bridge for later file opens. Keep this as a narrow opener script plus bridge-enabled yzn-hx; do not port main Yazelix pane orchestrator or full control plane.
notes: Final committed Rust helper shape: yzn-open keeps the bridge opener narrow with anyhow, serde, and serde_json only. Follow-up simplification removed fs-err and path-absolutize before commit; standard library filesystem/path APIs are enough for this Linux-only slice.
close_reason: Implemented with a Rust yzn-open helper. Yazi calls yzn-open; yzn-open reuses a live Yazelix Helix bridge via typed serde JSON over a Unix socket and falls back to opening yzn-hx in a right Zellij pane. Used anyhow, fs-err, path-absolutize, serde, and serde_json; skipped interprocess/duct/clap because they did not reduce this Linux-only slice enough. Verified cargo test, nix build, flake show, temp profile install, built Yazi debug, fallback smoke test, and refreshed installed yzn.

title: Verify yzn session and window isolation
description:
Check that separate yzn launches/windows are isolated from each other and do not accidentally share opener, Helix bridge, Zellij session, or Yazi state.

Context:
- yzn generates YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID at top-level launch.
- yzn-hx defaults the bridge session id to yzn when the env is absent.
- yzn-yazi also defaults the bridge session id to yzn when the env is absent.
- yzn-open scans ${YAZELIX_STATE_DIR}/helix_bridge/<session_id>/ and uses registry zellij_session_name matching when present.
- Multiple Mars/Zellij windows or externally invoked helpers could expose isolation bugs if env propagation or fallback defaults are wrong.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the expected isolation contract for one yzn window, multiple yzn windows, and helper commands invoked outside yzn.
- Verify whether yzn, yzn-yazi, yzn-hx, and yzn-open always agree on the intended session id inside a normal yzn window.
- Verify that opening files from Yazi in one yzn window cannot target a Helix bridge in another yzn window.
- Decide whether the yzn-yazi/yzn-hx fallback session id of yzn is acceptable or should fail/derive a safer id when launched outside yzn.
- Add the smallest focused tests or runtime inspections needed to prove the contract.
- Keep the README LOC scorecard updated if implementation changes files.
close_reason: Implemented and verified session/window isolation: documented the yzn bridge session contract, changed helper fallbacks away from shared literal yzn, propagated yzn-open's chosen session id when spawning editor panes, added Rust tests for fallback ids plus cross-yzn and cross-Zellij-session mismatch, and verified with cargo test, nix flake check, nix flake show, nix build, temp profile install, and nix profile upgrade.

title: Finalize the flake-installed yzn Mars launcher baseline
description:
## Context
Create the first Yazelix Next baseline as a Nix-compatible flake. The project should expose a short `yzn` command that opens Mars, support both `nix run` and profile installation from a local or absolute path, and keep a visible LOC scorecard so future work has a simplicity budget.

## Decisions
- Use Nix/Lix-compatible flakes as the project interface.
- Use `yzn` to avoid conflicting with the existing `yzx` command.
- Do not add Home Manager yet.
- Do not add Zellij or a workspace layer until the user explicitly chooses that next feature.
- Keep Mars packaging complexity isolated upstream.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `flake.nix` exposes `packages.${system}.yzn` and `packages.${system}.default` for supported Linux systems.
- `flake.nix` exposes `apps.${system}.yzn` and `apps.${system}.default` so `nix run` opens the launcher.
- The installed package provides `bin/yzn`, and `yzn` execs Mars without claiming the existing `yzx` command.
- README documents `nix run`, `nix run .#yzn`, absolute-path profile install with `--refresh`, and a per-language LOC scorecard.
- The LOC scorecard separates handwritten Markdown, handwritten Nix, and generated JSON lockfile lines.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` succeeds.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` succeeds.
- A temp profile install using `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` exposes `<tmp>/bin/yzn`.
- Human dogfooding confirms `yzn` opens a Mars window before the bead is closed.
close_reason: Completed by the staged baseline and superseding Zellij slice: flake exposes yzn/default apps and packages, README documents run/install/update and per-language LOC, nix flake show/build/temp profile install passed, and user confirmed yzn works.

title: Add Starship, Carapace, and zoxide to Nushell
description: Package Starship, Carapace, and zoxide into the Yazelix Next Nushell runtime, and configure the packaged default shell to use Starship prompt plus generated Carapace and zoxide Nushell init scripts.
acceptance_criteria:
A repo-owned Nu config wires Starship as the prompt.
The packaged Nu config sources generated Carapace and zoxide Nushell init scripts.
yzn default_shell points at a packaged Nushell wrapper that loads the repo-owned Nu config.
yzn runtime includes nushell, starship, carapace, and zoxide on PATH.
The installed package includes the Nu config and generated init scripts under share/yazelix-next.
nix flake show, Nu config/init smoke checks, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms the shell integration is working; Starship, generated Carapace init, generated zoxide init, banner suppression, package build, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Add Yazelix Helix opener
description: Package the Yazelix Helix fork in yazelix-next and configure the packaged Yazi sidebar to open files with it. Keep the slice narrow: no broad Helix config management, no Yazi config layering, and no pane-control bridge unless dogfooding proves it is needed.
notes: Final shape: yzn and yzn-yazi export EDITOR/VISUAL to the packaged yzn-hx wrapper, yzn-hx launches the Yazelix Helix fork with the repo-owned config dir, and packaged Yazi opens text files through Zellij native action edit so Zellij owns pane creation. Avoided direct Yazi-pane Helix launches and avoided a custom pane-control bridge.
close_reason: Implemented: yzn packages the Yazelix Helix fork; yzn-hx launches it with a repo-owned config dir; yzn and yzn-yazi export EDITOR/VISUAL to yzn-hx; packaged Yazi opens text files through Zellij native action edit so Zellij owns pane creation. Verified with nix build, flake show, Helix health, Yazi debug, temp profile install, and refreshed installed yzn.
